name: mfa
description: Multi-factor authentication challenge protocols. Composes on top of identity/identity's MFA secret storage â€” this module OWNS the CHALLENGE + VERIFICATION lifecycle; the identity module owns SECRET STORAGE. Ships three challenge types: TOTP (RFC 6238 time-based codes, 30s period, 6-digit, SHA1 HMAC), WebAuthn (FIDO2 platform authenticator or roaming hardware key â€” used as a second factor only in Wave 1a per spec Â§12 non-goals), and recovery codes (10 single-use codes, 10 chars from a Crockford base32 alphabet â‰ˆ 48 bits entropy per code, bcrypt-hashed at rest, plaintext returned ONCE at enrolment / regeneration). Every challenge transits the shared auth_mfa_challenges table (owned by identity/auth) â€” this module dispatches verification actions and manages device registrations (`webauthn_credentials` table). Enrolment flow is two-round: preview (generate secret, return once for QR display) â†’ confirm (submit secret + verification code to prove the scan). Recovery codes are generated at first enrolment; consumable by any subsequent MFA challenge as a fallback when the TOTP device is unavailable. Constant-time comparisons throughout (`hash_equals()` for recovery codes, timing-safe TOTP window comparison, `hash_equals()` on the WebAuthn assertion challenge). KMS-backed secret encryption is delegated to identity/identity via `MfaSecretEncryptor`. This module is the PROTOCOL layer; identity/identity is the STORAGE substrate.

packages: web-auth/webauthn-lib (FIDO2 attestation + assertion verification per W3C Level 2 spec)
wraps.rationale: We do not reimplement RFC 6238 or the W3C WebAuthn spec â€” we wrap the vetted community packages inside our `TotpChallenger` / `WebauthnAssertionVerifier` / `WebauthnAttestationVerifier` services. The wrappers add (a) `hash_equals()`-based constant-time comparisons around the third-party output, (b) rate-limit + challenge-ledger integration via identity/auth's `auth_mfa_challenges` table, (c) `MfaChallengeFailed` / `MfaChallengeSucceeded` domain events, and (d) sign-count monotonic replay-protection on WebAuthn credentials.

tenant.description: Tenant-audience MFA self-service surface. Every route requires an authenticated Sanctum PAT and an active tenant context.

platform-admin.description: Platform-admin surface for support + emergency operations. Every write path is audit-critical and requires a `--reason` argument (or JSON `reason` field).

identity_module_owns: Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted (the encrypted TOTP shared secret at rest)
Identity.mfa_recovery_codes_hashed (bcrypt-hashed recovery codes in a JSONB array on the Identity row)
MfaSecretEncryptor service (KMS envelope encryption / decryption / rotation)
MfaEnrolled + MfaDisabled events (fired ON the Identity row when enrolment is confirmed OR fully disabled)
mfa_module_owns: Challenge dispatch across TOTP / WebAuthn / recovery-code protocols
TOTP verification (RFC 6238 window comparison, drift tolerance)
Recovery-code consumption protocol (constant-time compare + atomic single-use removal)
The `/me/mfa/*` HTTP surface (tenant self-service) + `/platform/mfa/*` HTTP surface (support tooling)
co_ownership_boundary.shared_table_note: The `auth_mfa_challenges` table is OWNED BY identity/auth (it's the challenge ledger for every authentication flow, not just MFA). This module dispatches CREATE / VERIFY / EXPIRE operations against that table via identity/auth's `MfaChallengeRepository` contract but does NOT own the table's schema or lifecycle.
